storyInfo: Windscoot tries to help the bandits who raised him go straight by starting an escort agency, leading to an acrimonious split amongst the bandits. On their first delivery mission, the new escort agency finds themselves facing off against their former comrades.

attributes.content: An escort car charges through the mountain roads of Shangshu; the violent rumbling of its engine is the only sound in the quiet mountains.
Where did you get that drink, Uncle De?!
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: You want a sip too? Just don't drink it all, it's a long trip.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: I hid it on purpose before we left. We can't drink on the job according to the rules...
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: The rules? Hah.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: ...If there's something you want to say, Uncle De, just say it.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: I didn't say it in the camp because I didn't want to make you lose face. You came back to the camp after running away for a few years, and the first thing you say is to tell us to stop being bandits and start an escort agency.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: A gang of so many years, split right down the middle and coming to blows because of your words.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: I didn't expect Aunt Wen to be so firm and leave with the others... but I didn't expect half of the guys to be willing to stay for the escort business either.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Did you think we stayed so that you could make a bunch of rules for us? You're just making life miserable for everyone.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: I tell you, there's only ever been one rule in the jianghu all these years—whoever has the biggest fist, makes the rules. Right, guys?
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: Some of the escorts nod in affirmation, while others keep quiet, observing the situation silently. They were bandits just a few days ago, and they still very much look like bandits.
...Why did you stay, Uncle De?
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: What's that, you wanna run me out?
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: No... I didn't want things to come to this. At this point, though, even if we wanted to go back to the Gang of Justice, Aunt Wen won't let us.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: You brat...
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: There's only one way out for us. All the more important that we should talk about it.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: I'm sure the others are of the same mind. If we can get this escort agency off the ground, we'll be able to live a more stable life from now on.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Pfft, you think that's so easy?
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: The hardest part is letting go of the past. You know how important an escort agency's first-ever mission is.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: ...Hmph. I remember how Wu Jinbiao made a few enemies in the jianghu, and his enemies robbed his cargo on his first mission, making sure that everyone knew about it... needless to say, he went out of business.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: An escort agency started by former bandits will always draw attention. Our 'old friends' are all rooting for us to fail.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: The moment we fail, we'll lose all credibility... our agency, the new lives that we wanted, all gone.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Gang of Justice has made plenty of enemies in the past. The mountains ahead are uninhabited. There's no harm in being careful.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Oh, shut it. Who d'ya think you are after just a few years in the jianghu? You were cuter when you were little.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: Gai si! What just happened?!
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: Stop!
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Keep the doors closed.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Several pairs of eyes scan the hills and forests, opening the doors only after making sure that there is no ambush.
Wo kao Two tires out!
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: A huge piece of glass under the leaves... this is Flying Feather Gang turf. But we already paid for safe passage.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Did they decide to up the price? Or is it someone else intruding on their turf?
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Anyway, we should get out of here. I'll call the nearest repair shop...
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Forget it. There's no signal out here in nowhere. That's the whole point of setting an ambush, so that there's no one you can call. Let's get someone out to seek help.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: No, splitting up is exactly what our enemy wants. We need to stick together. Let's sit tight; I doubt the other side wants to wait an entire night.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: He leans back against the car, his gaze fixed on the dark woods.
It takes little time before a rough-looking group emerges from the darkness and surrounds the convoy. They are carrying the same ring-hilted swords that the escorts are.
The middle-aged woman that leads the gang makes a gesture, and the bandits draw their swords.
You're not stupid.
...I knew it.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: ...Aunt Wen.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Aunt Wen! Guys! I'm back!
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: ...Tsk.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: It's me, Cheng. Don't you recognize me?
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: You were a pretty little baby boy when you left. Now you look like one of those stinking 'gentlemen'.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Haha, stop making fun of him, Wen. Shall I tell him how many words you learned in order to read his letters, or the cystybeast that you saved for today?
attributes.name: Jolly Gang Member

attributes.content: Shut up. Those who fail to follow the rules and leave the gang on their own must face punishment.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: I'll hold my sword for now, since you came back on your own. Go admit your mistakes to the leaders of the past.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Do we have to do this? He just came back... isn't it enough that he came back?
attributes.name: Jolly Gang Member

attributes.content: Go.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Ok. For how long?
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Don't play the fool.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Mm.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Leaders of the Gang of Justice. The uncles and aunts sent me out to study at a school, but I was expelled and never came back because I was ashamed to face them.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: I missed everyone, but I didn't want to go back until I've made a name of my own, so I stayed away all these years.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: But I sincerely hope that I can help everyone this time, repay them for raising me, and make their lives better... please don't be angry with me.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Windscoot is standing in front of the plaques of the past leaders, but his head is turned around to look at Aunt Wen.
(Sighs)
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Why did you come back?
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: To help.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Help what?
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Our new business. It may not look that way, but I've done a lot of work these few years, including working as an escort. I heard that we just started an escort agency, and I can help with anything you need.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Who told you we were starting an escort agency?
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Er... jianghu gossip? Was I mistaken?
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: You didn't tell him, Wen?
attributes.name: Jolly Gang Member

attributes.content: Shut up.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: ...What?
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: ...You don't need to know. I'm busy. You reflect on your mistakes and come have dinner once you're done.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: So, this is the truth about this 'escort agency' business. It's a sensitive issue. Don't ask the leader about it, especially not in front of everyone else...
attributes.name: Jolly Gang Member

attributes.content: You're telling me that the ads we put out were fake, just trying to scam people out of their cargo? Maybe it works this time, but what about after that?
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: And what about it? Time's hard, and we've got our hands full making ends meet right now. We'll think about the future when it comes.
attributes.name: Jolly Gang Member
Jolly Gang Member
attributes.content: And stop acting like you're the paragon of justice. Don't tell me you forgot what the Gang of Justice's line of work is after a few years outside.

attributes.name: Jolly Gang Member
attributes.content: Hey, where are you going?
attributes.joined: And stop acting like you're the paragon of justice. Don't tell me you forgot what the Gang of Justice's line of work is after a few years outside. Hey, where are you going?

attributes.content: Aunt Wen! I would like a word.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Have you reflected on your mistakes?
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Fine. You have something to say, you can say it here. You only just came back. What unrest you can feel, the others can too.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Aunt Wen is talking to Windscoot, but her eyes are on the others in the gang with dissent on their faces.

Windscoot is at a loss for words. He senses that something feels off about the hall. In his memories, the camp is always filled with laughter, but there seems to be something invisible meandering through the room, separating them from one another.
...Please, reconsider the matter of the escort agency.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: I didn't want to make things difficult for you. It's not my place to criticize when I haven't been around to endure the hard times together. But I did ask around a little on my way back.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: I found that people outside were not so resistant to the idea of former bandits opening an escort agency, and we even happen to get a job. What if we did the job properly?
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Maybe this time it doesn't pay well, but once we make our name out there, there'll be more in the future...
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Aunt Wen knocks over the coin box next to her to the gasps and surprised cries of the others in the room. Then, the ones who looked restless show hesitation—there are only a few silver coins in the box.
Future? You feed every mouth in this place before you talk to me about the future.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Things have gotten this bad? Then the cystybeast in the backyard was saved for when I came back...
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Shut your mouth if you understand. Besides, there's no need to talk about this now.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Our guys have already done it?
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Aunt Wen stares at the boy whom she had raised, her expression unreadable. The young man hesitates because he does not want to embarrass her, but there are words that must be said, things that must be done.
If life has become so hard in our old line of work, then it's all the more reason for us to look for a different way out. Where are they?
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Why do you ask?
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Trust is hard to establish but easy to break. There's no way back once the robbery is committed. I'll get the cargo back first, then we can talk about this.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: The decision is already made. You want to change it, you'll have to do it by force.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Aunt Wen!
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: The hall is filled with the sound of swords being drawn from their sheaths. Virtually everyone is holding a weapon by the time Windscoot can react.
Some swords are raised in defense of their leader, others against her.
Stop! Calm down!
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: I'm calm. Tell us what you plan to do once you get the cargo back, kid.
attributes.name: Observative Gang Member

attributes.content: (Looks hesitatingly at Aunt Wen)
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Alright, I guess this is my chance to talk.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: We'll get the cargo back and deliver it to the customer normally, get the job out of the way.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Then we can start a shop. Accepting orders, filing documents, planning routes... I know a bit about how the whole process works.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: What about the cost of rent and equipment?
attributes.name: Observative Gang Member

attributes.content: I saved a little over the years. If everyone chips in a little...
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Fine, as long as you know how to do it. Keep going. Is it gonna work?
attributes.name: Observative Gang Member

attributes.content: I can't guarantee anything. There are a lot of people who fail. It won't be easy, and there's no quick money.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: We'll have to change our habits of solving problems through violence. We'll have to refrain from fighting and killing if we want to keep going.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Hah. I know these people better than you do. You're sending them down a dead-end path.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: I would be more suspicious if you said it would be easy.
attributes.name: Observative Gang Member

attributes.content: I'll take it, Cheng. I'll tell you where the cargo is, and you find us a new path.
attributes.name: Observative Gang Member

attributes.content: No need to worry about those who try to stop you.
attributes.name: Observative Gang Member

attributes.content: Windscoot finally delivered the cargo and returned to the Gang of Justice to find it in shambles.
Aunt Wen had left with half of the gang. The rest have cooked the cystybeast, but no one is in the mood for meat in the ruined camp.
This is the first time the two halves of the gang have met again since the split.
Aunt Wen... how is everyone? I heard things got pretty violent that day...
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: ...Can you let us through?
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Did you think we were waiting here to listen to you say that?
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: You know what the Gang of Justice does, and you don't need me to tell you how we deal with people who don't follow the rules.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: I won't draw my sword, and I won't give you the cargo. I just ask that you let us through, for the sake of old times.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: This is our first job since we opened for business. We invited everyone we could. Lose the cargo, and we'll have nothing left.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: You swore you would find a way for everyone, but you can't even make your way through us?
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Make our way we will! You think we're afraid? You can't find your way, so you don't want others to do so either? We'll kill whoever stands in our way!
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.text: 'Kill! Kill!' The other escorts start yelling angrily. The bandits yell back, and the two sides look ready to kill at any moment.
Windscoot stands between them, his voice hoarse from shouting.
It is the same thing again, the two sides drawing swords against each other at the first argument. Windscoot seems to see a smile on Aunt Wen's face.
She looks at Windscoot with an expression of 'I told you so.'

attributes.content: Calm down, Uncle De.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Gan ta de! I'll show them—
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: Apologies.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Are you out of your mind? Cao! So cold! Why did you pour that damn drink on my head, you brat?!
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: Alright, now that we're quiet, hear me out.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: We don't solve problems by talking.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Things aren't what they used to be. At least, what we want now is different from before.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: No, even before...
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: You can start going to school tomorrow. You'll taste the fists of all your aunts and uncles if you don't study.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: I don't wanna go to school.
attributes.name: Young Cheng

attributes.content: Say that again!
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: Why did you have to beg the teacher like that? You said whoever has the biggest fist makes the rules. Is the teacher's fist bigger than yours? Ouch! Why did you hit me?
attributes.name: Young Cheng

attributes.content: Don't let your Aunt Wen hear that, brat.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: Aunt Wen never bowed to anyone...
attributes.name: Young Cheng

attributes.content: Besides, I heard them say...
attributes.name: Young Cheng

attributes.text: The child of a musbeast will be a musbeast.

attributes.content: Never mind. Anyway, I don't wanna go. I want to live with my head high like you guys—ouch! Let go of my ear!
attributes.name: Young Cheng

attributes.content: Listen, kid. You can't avoid getting punched in the mouth in this line of work. Get out of this camp if you want to live with your head high.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: You know how many people around here couldn't get out even if they wanted? Those who lost everything after being scammed out of their land, those who stood up for justice and ended up getting their whole family killed... they have to rely on the protection of your Aunt Wen.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: But you're different. You still have hope.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: What about you? When will you get out?
attributes.name: Young Cheng

attributes.content: Windscoot does not remember Uncle De's answer. Probably made a casual joke, as usual.
This time, however, Uncle De took his side without hesitation. And he understood: it was not that they did not want to; it was that they did not know how.
Someone has to be the first to walk the path and prove that it leads somewhere.
Windscoot makes up his mind. He reaches toward his belt. Aunt Wen has her eyes on his sword, as does everyone else. At this range, a single move determines life and death.

As Windscoot's fingers touch his belt, Aunt Wen clutches the hilt of her sword, only to see Windscoot take out a small stack of banknotes, put it between his fingers, and present it to Aunt Wen, an old jianghu ritual.
Aunt Wen, Uncle De. You found me in the wilds, but raised me like I was your own son, even sending me to school.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: You wanted the same thing for me then as I want for everyone now.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Some of us may find it hard to quit the bandit life, but others can scout the path first to see if it works. Give us a chance, Aunt Wen.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: In formal terms...
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: The Escort Agency of Justice wants to make friends with the Gang of Justice. In the name of friendship, we share what we have, and ask that you grant passage.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Did you think a little bit of money can smooth over everything?
attributes.name: Angry Gang Member

attributes.content: No, I know that those who violate the rules must face punishment. But I was the one who stirred up the troubles, and I should be the one to bleed for it. After that, I ask that the Gang of Justice accept this... little token from the Escort Agency of Justice.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Windscoot stretches out his other arm.
Hey!
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: (Shakes his head at Uncle De)
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Go ahead, Boss Wen.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Very well, very well. 'We,' 'you.' Very well.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Very well!
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: Argh!
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Instead of cutting off Windscoot's arm, the sword slams down on Windscoot's forehead, still inside its sheath. Blood begins flowing from the cut.
The sword hits the ground with a clang, but Aunt Wen makes no show of picking it up.
We are strangers from this day forth! Let's go.
attributes.name: Aunt Wen

attributes.content: You alright? No concussion or anything? That was one hard hit.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: They're really gone.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: Windscoot picks up Aunt Wen's sword, wipes the dirt off it, and wears it on his belt next to his own sword.
She held something back... I think there will come a day when I give this sword back to her.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Ouch! I wanted you to bandage up the wound, not open it up!
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Oh...
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: What is it?
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: The sun's coming up.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: Let's go. We still have our first cargo to deliver.
attributes.name: Windscoot

attributes.content: We gotta repair the tires first, brat.
attributes.name: Uncle De

attributes.content: Oops, almost forgot.
attributes.name: Windscoot
